name: macro_simple
grammar:
S <- HELLO WORLD
HELLO <- T('hello')
WORLD <- T('world')
T(a) <- a [ \t]*

name: macro_two_parameters
grammar:
S <- HELLO_WORLD
HELLO_WORLD <- T('hello', 'world')
T(a, b) <- a [ \t]* b [ \t]*

name: macro_syntax_error
description: Space between macro name and parameter list is invalid

name: macro_missing_argument
description: Calling macro with wrong number of arguments fails
grammar:
S <- T ('hello')
T(a, b) <- a [ \t]* b

name: macro_reference_syntax_error
description: Space before macro arguments in reference is invalid

name: macro_invalid_reference
description: Calling a non-macro rule with arguments fails

name: macro_expression_arguments
description: Macro arguments can be expressions with choice
grammar:
S <- M('hello' / 'Hello', 'world' / 'World')
M(arg0, arg1) <- arg0 [ \t]+ arg1

name: macro_exclusive_modifiers
description: Macro used for exclusive modifier tracking (each modifier allowed once)
grammar:
S <- Modifiers(!"") _
Modifiers(Appeared) <- (!Appeared) (
  Token('public') Modifiers(Appeared / 'public') /
  Token('static') Modifiers(Appeared / 'static') /
  Token('final') Modifiers(Appeared / 'final') /
  "")
Token(t) <- t _
_ <- [ \t\r\n]*

name: macro_passes_arg_to_another_macro
description: Macro passing its parameter to another macro

name: nested_macro_call
grammar:
A <- B(T)
B(X) <- C(X)
C(Y) <- Y
T <- 'val'

name: nested_macro_call2
grammar:
START <- A('TestVal1', 'TestVal2')+
A(Aarg1, Aarg2) <- B(Aarg1) '#End'
B(Barg1) <- '#' Barg1

name: macro_with_complex_arguments
description: Macro with literal arguments forming key=value pair
grammar:
S <- PAIR('key', 'value')
PAIR(K, V) <- K '=' V
